description_long: This is a large, conical bottle with a rounded body tapering to a narrow neck and a small, slightly raised mouth. The surface is matte and appears to be made of ceramic or stoneware, with visible discoloration and stains suggesting age or outdoor exposure. The bottle is predominantly a pale beige or off-white color, with some orange-brown patches and darker marks scattered across its surface. The base is flat and slightly wider, providing stability, while the neck is short and stubby, lacking any visible handle or spout. There are no visible labels or branding, but some faint text or markings are present on the bottom. The overall form and finish suggest it could be an old storage or transport vessel, possibly for liquids.
description: A large, conical, beige ceramic bottle with a matte, weathered surface and visible stains and marks. It has a short neck and a flat base, with no visible labels or branding.
description_view_0: View from the mouth, showing the small, circular opening and the slightly raised rim, with the body receding away.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated, conical shape and the pale, stained surface with some orange-brown discoloration.
description_view_2: View from the base, showing the flat bottom with faint text or markings and some concentrated discoloration.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, displaying the bottle's full length and the short neck, with more visible surface stains.
attribute_label_or_branding: Faint text or markings on the bottom; no visible labels or branding elsewhere.
attribute_damage_or_deformation: Visible discoloration and stains; no major cracks, dents, or structural damage.

license_info.creator_display_name: LDGP Geometric Documentation of Heritage Lab.
